# Core Inventory Management PRP - BasicAgInventory
<!-- Generated by CODEX Enhanced PRP Creation - Complete Workflow Context Integration -->

## Goal

**Feature Goal**: Enable farmers to digitally manage farm inventory (equipment, supplies, feed, medications) with full CRUD operations, supporting offline-first operations and real-time quantity updates across multiple farm locations, reducing inventory management time by 30% while preventing costly stockouts.

**Deliverable**: SwiftUI-based inventory management system with Core Data persistence, implementing MVVM architecture pattern with ObservableObject ViewModels and repository pattern for data access.

**Success Definition**:
- Users can add, edit, delete inventory items in under 30 seconds per operation
- Inventory quantity updates work offline with automatic sync when connected
- Search results appear within 1 second for databases up to 10,000 items
- 99.5% crash-free rate with AES-256 encrypted local storage

## User Persona

**Target User**: Farm Manager Sarah - Owner/operator of 200-acre crop and livestock farm

**Use Case**: Daily inventory tracking and management during farm operations, often in outdoor environments with limited connectivity

**User Journey**:
1. Launch app → Dashboard view (< 3 seconds)
2. Search/browse to find item (< 1 second results)
3. Update quantities via tap controls or manual entry
4. Move items between farm locations
5. Set minimum stock levels for alerts
6. View inventory distributed across all locations

**Pain Points Addressed**:
- Limited time for complex interfaces
- Need for offline access in rural areas
- Prevention of emergency purchases due to stockouts
- Tracking inventory across multiple farm locations

## Why

**Business Value**:
- Reduces weekly inventory management time from 2 hours to 45 minutes
- Prevents 90% of emergency purchases through proactive stock monitoring
- Enables data-driven purchasing decisions with usage pattern analysis
- Supports regulatory compliance for agricultural record-keeping

**Technical Requirements**:
- FR1: Add, edit, delete inventory items with categories and detailed metadata
- FR2: Update quantities through increment/decrement or manual entry
- FR3: Search and filter with instant results (<1 second)
- FR4: Multi-location tracking with GPS coordinates
- FR8: Transfer items between locations with transaction history

**Architecture Decisions**:
- SwiftUI + MVVM pattern for reactive UI updates
- Core Data with SQLite encryption for offline-first data persistence
- Repository pattern for data access abstraction
- ObservableObject ViewModels with @Published properties for state management

**Integration Needs**:
- Core Location Services for GPS coordinates
- iOS Camera framework for future barcode scanning
- Apple Push Notifications for low stock alerts

## What

This implementation creates a comprehensive inventory management system using SwiftUI's declarative UI framework with MVVM architecture. The system provides full CRUD operations for inventory items, supporting offline-first operations through Core Data persistence with automatic conflict resolution and transaction logging.

**Core Features**:
- **Item Management**: Add, edit, delete inventory items with rich metadata (name, category, quantity, unit, location, purchase date, photos)
- **Quantity Tracking**: Real-time quantity updates with increment/decrement controls and manual entry
- **Multi-Location Support**: Track items across farm locations with GPS coordinates and transfer capabilities
- **Search & Filtering**: Instant search with results filtering by category, location, name, and availability status
- **Offline Operations**: Full functionality without internet connectivity using Core Data local storage
- **Transaction History**: Complete audit trail for all inventory changes with timestamps and user tracking

### Success Criteria

- [ ] App launches in under 3 seconds on iPhone 7+ devices
- [ ] Add new inventory item workflow completes in under 30 seconds
- [ ] Inventory search returns results within 1 second for 10,000+ items
- [ ] Quantity updates save locally and sync when connection available
- [ ] All data encrypted at rest using AES-256 via Core Data encryption
- [ ] Supports offline mode with full read/write capability
- [ ] 99.5% crash-free rate across supported iOS devices
- [ ] WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility compliance with VoiceOver support

### Known Gotchas & Constraints

```swift
// CRITICAL: SwiftUI and Core Data integration constraints from architecture

// SwiftUI Threading Requirements:
// - All UI updates must happen on MainActor
// - Core Data operations should use background contexts for performance
@MainActor
class InventoryListViewModel: ObservableObject {
    // Must use @Published for SwiftUI reactivity
    @Published private(set) var inventoryItems: [InventoryItem] = []
}

// Core Data Performance Constraints:
// - Batch operations limited to 5000 objects for memory management
// - NSFetchedResultsController required for large datasets (10,000+ items)
// - Must use faulting for memory-efficient lazy loading

// From Architecture Security Requirements:
// - AES-256 encryption must be enabled via Core Data SQLite store options
// - No sensitive data in UserDefaults - use Keychain Services only
// - All user input must be validated and sanitized

// From PRD Performance Requirements (NFR1-NFR4):
// - App launch time < 3 seconds - avoid heavy initialization on main thread
// - Search results < 1 second - use NSPredicate with efficient Core Data queries
// - Offline operations required - no network dependency for core functionality

// From Architecture Error Handling:
// - Use Swift Result type for all repository operations
// - Custom error enums for domain-specific failures
// - User-friendly error messages with recovery guidance
```

## Implementation Blueprint

### Data Models and Structure

```swift
// Core entities from PRD data requirements section
// Following architecture Core Data + SwiftUI integration patterns

import CoreData
import Foundation

// Main inventory item entity - supports up to 10,000 items per farm
@objc(InventoryItem)
public class InventoryItem: NSManagedObject {
    @NSManaged public var id: UUID
    @NSManaged public var name: String
    @NSManaged public var itemDescription: String
    @NSManaged public var category: String
    @NSManaged public var quantity: Int32
    @NSManaged public var unitOfMeasure: String
    @NSManaged public var minimumStock: Int32
    @NSManaged public var purchaseDate: Date?
    @NSManaged public var purchasePrice: Decimal
    @NSManaged public var createdAt: Date
    @NSManaged public var updatedAt: Date

    // Relationships from architecture entity model
    @NSManaged public var location: Location?
    @NSManaged public var transactions: NSSet?

    // Computed properties for SwiftUI
    var isLowStock: Bool {
        quantity <= minimumStock
    }

    // Validation from PRD business rules
    func validate() throws {
        guard !name.trimmingCharacters(in: .whitespacesAndNewlines).isEmpty else {
            throw InventoryError.invalidName
        }
        guard quantity >= 0 else {
            throw InventoryError.invalidQuantity
        }
        guard minimumStock >= 0 else {
            throw InventoryError.invalidMinimumStock
        }
    }
}

// Location entity for multi-location tracking (FR7-FR10)
@objc(Location)
public class Location: NSManagedObject {
    @NSManaged public var id: UUID
    @NSManaged public var name: String
    @NSManaged public var locationDescription: String
    @NSManaged public var latitude: Double
    @NSManaged public var longitude: Double
    @NSManaged public var createdAt: Date

    @NSManaged public var inventoryItems: NSSet?
}

// Transaction audit trail entity
@objc(Transaction)
public class Transaction: NSManagedObject {
    @NSManaged public var id: UUID
    @NSManaged public var type: String // "add", "update", "move", "delete"
    @NSManaged public var previousQuantity: Int32
    @NSManaged public var newQuantity: Int32
    @NSManaged public var notes: String?
    @NSManaged public var timestamp: Date

    @NSManaged public var inventoryItem: InventoryItem?
    @NSManaged public var fromLocation: Location?
    @NSManaged public var toLocation: Location?
}

// Domain-specific error handling
enum InventoryError: Error, LocalizedError {
    case invalidName
    case invalidQuantity
    case invalidMinimumStock
    case locationNotFound
    case insufficientQuantity

    var errorDescription: String? {
        switch self {
        case .invalidName:
            return "Item name cannot be empty"
        case .invalidQuantity:
            return "Quantity must be zero or greater"
        case .invalidMinimumStock:
            return "Minimum stock must be zero or greater"
        case .locationNotFound:
            return "Selected location not found"
        case .insufficientQuantity:
            return "Not enough items available for this operation"
        }
    }
}
```

```yaml
Task 9: INTEGRATE LocationService with Core Location framework

Task 10: IMPLEMENT Low Stock Alert System

  - REQUIREMENT: Push notifications when quantities fall below thresholds
  - ARCHITECTURE: Apple Push Notification Service integration
  - SECURITY: Local notification fallback for privacy
  - VALIDATION: Alert accuracy and timing verification
```

### Implementation Patterns & Key Details

```swift
// SwiftUI + Core Data integration pattern from architecture.md
// MVVM pattern with ObservableObject ViewModels

// Repository Pattern (Core/Data/Repositories/InventoryRepository.swift)
protocol InventoryRepositoryProtocol {
    func fetchAllItems() async throws -> [InventoryItem]
    func fetchItems(matching searchTerm: String) async throws -> [InventoryItem]
    func fetchItems(in location: Location) async throws -> [InventoryItem]
    func createItem(_ item: InventoryItem) async throws
    func updateItem(_ item: InventoryItem) async throws
    func deleteItem(_ item: InventoryItem) async throws
    func updateQuantity(for item: InventoryItem, newQuantity: Int32) async throws
    func moveItem(_ item: InventoryItem, to location: Location, quantity: Int32) async throws
}

class CoreDataInventoryRepository: InventoryRepositoryProtocol {
    private let context: NSManagedObjectContext

    init(context: NSManagedObjectContext) {
        self.context = context
    }

    // Efficient search with NSPredicate for <1 second results (NFR2)
    func fetchItems(matching searchTerm: String) async throws -> [InventoryItem] {
        let request: NSFetchRequest<InventoryItem> = InventoryItem.fetchRequest()
        request.predicate = NSPredicate(format: "name CONTAINS[cd] %@ OR itemDescription CONTAINS[cd] %@",
                                       searchTerm, searchTerm)
        request.sortDescriptors = [NSSortDescriptor(keyPath: \InventoryItem.name, ascending: true)]

        return try await context.perform {
            try self.context.fetch(request)
        }
    }
}

// ViewModel Pattern (Features/Inventory/ViewModels/InventoryListViewModel.swift)
@MainActor
class InventoryListViewModel: ObservableObject {
    @Published private(set) var inventoryItems: [InventoryItem] = []
    @Published private(set) var lowStockItems: [InventoryItem] = []
    @Published var searchText: String = ""
    @Published private(set) var isLoading: Bool = false
    @Published private(set) var error: Error?

    private let repository: InventoryRepositoryProtocol

    init(repository: InventoryRepositoryProtocol) {
        self.repository = repository
    }

    // Search with debouncing for performance
    func searchItems() async {
        isLoading = true
        defer { isLoading = false }

        do {
            inventoryItems = try await repository.fetchItems(matching: searchText)
        } catch {
            self.error = error
        }
    }
}

// Error Handling Pattern (from architecture coding standards)
enum InventoryViewError: Error, LocalizedError {
    case loadingFailed
    case savingFailed
    case networkUnavailable

    var errorDescription: String? {
        switch self {
        case .loadingFailed:
            return "Unable to load inventory items. Please try again."
        case .savingFailed:
            return "Failed to save changes. Your data is safe locally."
        case .networkUnavailable:
            return "Working offline. Changes will sync when connected."
        }
    }
}

// SwiftUI View Pattern with accessibility
struct InventoryListView: View {
    @StateObject private var viewModel: InventoryListViewModel
    @State private var showingAddItem = false

    init(viewModel: InventoryListViewModel) {
        self._viewModel = StateObject(wrappedValue: viewModel)
    }

    var body: some View {
        NavigationView {
            VStack {
                SearchBar(text: $viewModel.searchText, onSearchButtonClicked: {
                    Task {
                        await viewModel.searchItems()
                    }
                })
                .accessibilityLabel("Search inventory items")

                List(viewModel.inventoryItems) { item in
                    InventoryItemRow(item: item)
                        .accessibilityElement(children: .combine)
                }
                .refreshable {
                    await viewModel.loadItems()
                }
            }
            .navigationTitle("Inventory")
            .toolbar {
                ToolbarItem(placement: .navigationBarTrailing) {
                    Button("Add") {
                        showingAddItem = true
                    }
                    .accessibilityLabel("Add new inventory item")
                }
            }
        }
        .sheet(isPresented: $showingAddItem) {
            AddInventoryView()
        }
    }
}
```

### Integration Points

```yaml
INTERNAL_INTEGRATION:
  - component: CoreDataManager
    interface: NSManagedObjectContext providing thread-safe data operations
    data_flow: ViewModels → Repository → Core Data → SQLite with encryption
    error_handling: Result type propagation with user-friendly error messages

  - component: LocationService
    interface: CLLocationManager delegate pattern for GPS coordinates
    data_flow: Location updates → Core Location → Location entities in Core Data
    error_handling: Permission denial graceful fallback to manual location entry

EXTERNAL_INTEGRATION:
  - service: Apple Push Notification Service (APNs)
    protocol: iOS Push Notifications framework for low stock alerts
    authentication: App-specific push certificates and device tokens
    fallback: Local notifications when remote notifications unavailable

  - service: iOS Core Location Services
    protocol: CLLocationManager for GPS coordinate capture
    authentication: User location permission with usage description
    fallback: Manual location name entry when location services denied

STATE_MANAGEMENT:
  - pattern: SwiftUI MVVM with ObservableObject ViewModels and @Published properties
  - persistence: Core Data with SQLite backend and AES-256 encryption
  - synchronization: Offline-first with eventual consistency (Phase 2: CloudKit sync)
```

## Anti-Patterns to Avoid

- ❌ Don't perform Core Data operations on the main thread - use background contexts
- ❌ Don't ignore offline-first requirement - all features must work without network
- ❌ Don't bypass input validation - sanitize all user input per security requirements
- ❌ Don't violate MVVM layer boundaries - Views should not directly access repositories
- ❌ Don't skip accessibility implementation - VoiceOver support is required
- ❌ Don't implement without proper error handling - use Result types consistently
- ❌ Don't ignore performance requirements - search must be <1 second
- ❌ Don't store sensitive data in UserDefaults - use encrypted Core Data only

High confidence based on complete context synthesis from all workflow documents, specific architectural guidance, and comprehensive implementation blueprint with validation strategy.
